label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the options A, B, C, or D is true based on the given text. First, let me read the passage again carefully. The passage starts by talking about computers and their uses. It says computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines. People use calculators for simple math, scientists use large computers to solve many problems at once. Then it mentions that you can play games with computers, some help learn languages. The Kurzweil Reading Machine is a special computer that reads books for blind people. Then it explains how the Kurzweil machine works: a blind person opens the book, puts it on the scanner, the machine reads the words. When the page is done, the person turns the page and puts it back. The machine can read in a normal or fast voice. The next sentence is cut off but says something about reading words with small and large letters. Then the text says blind people like the Kurzweil machine because they can "read" anything with it: newspapers, magazines, books, etc. Libraries have these machines, and it can also help short-sighted children learn to read. Now the question is which of the four options is true. Option A: Computers can solve all the problems people can't do. The passage says computers can solve difficult problems, but does it say they can solve all problems people can't? The original text says "Computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines." However, the option says "all the problems people can't do." That's a strong statement. The passage doesn't say computers can do all problems people can't, just that they can solve difficult problems. So A might be incorrect. Option B: Large computers can only solve one problem at a time. The passage states "Scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time." So large computers can solve many problems at once. Option B says they can "only solve one problem at a time," which is the opposite of what the passage says. So B is definitely wrong. Option C: When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything. The passage says the blind person "can simply open the book and put it down on the scanner. The machine 'reads' the words on the page. When one page is finished, the blind person turns the page and puts the book on the scanner again." So the blind person has to open the book, place it on the scanner, turn the page and put it back. So they do have to do some actions. Therefore, C is not true because the blind person does need to do things. Option D: The
